{{Quote|Around the time of the Great Hyperspace War, a Sith Lord named Garu lost King Adas's Holocron on the planet Ashas Ree, where it remained until it was found centuries later by Freedon Nadd.|Tionne Solusar, ''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force''|<ref name="Jedi" />}}
'''Garu''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] of the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]] who lived during the Empire's final [[Standard year/Legends|years]] and fought in the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]].<ref name="swcw">''[[SkyeWalkers: A Clone Wars Story]]''</ref> A [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Korriz]], Garu was the rival of the Sith Lord known as the [[Patron]].<ref name="CSWE" />

{{RPGseed}}
Garu commissioned [[Tritos Nal]], a master of [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]] and [[Sith alchemy/Legends|alchemy]], to construct him a powerful [[Sith sword]] for him. However, the sword, which became known as [[Garu's Warblade]], was stolen by agents of Garu's rival, the Sith Lord known as the Patron. In [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]], Garu sent a minor battle [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] to capture the ''[[Starbreaker 12]]''—a [[Scout/Legends|scout]] [[Starship/Legends|ship]] from the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] that had been discovered in [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] space—but the Patron's agents interfered and prevented Garu from securing the ship.<ref name="Dark Side" />
{{Endgame}}

Garu was one of the last known Sith Lords to be in possession of the [[Holocron of King Adas]],<ref name="Evil">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/88/indexp2.html|text=''Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties''|int=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties}}</ref> though Garu's [[Death/Legends|death]] on the planet [[Ashas Ree/Legends|Ashas Ree]]<ref name="Jedi" /> during the Great Hyperspace War that destroyed the Empire in 5000 BBY saw the disappearance of the [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]].<ref name="CSWE" /> Eventually, Garu's Warblade was broken into pieces and scattered across the [[Esstran sector/Legends|Esstran sector]] by the time of the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Empire]].<ref name="Gather">{{TORcite|type=crewskill|Treasure Hunting|Gather the Pieces}}</ref>

During the [[New Sith Wars]], [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]] member [[Seviss Vaa]] researched the worlds of the Sith Empire, and claimed that Garu lost or abandoned [[Sith King|King]] [[Adas]]'s holocron just prior to the Great Hyperspace War in his recordings in the [[Telos Holocron]]. The contents of the holocron were later published as part of the compendium ''[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force]]'' written by the Jedi Master [[Tionne Solusar]] in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]].<ref name="Jedi" />

Long after his death, the monstrosity [[Zeta Magnus]] took the alias ''Saturna of Garu'' in reference to the long dead Sith Lord. [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Halagad Ventor]] connected the names which led him to believe that the title had influenced the [[Dantari]] legend of a shaman called [[Garoo]] on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]].<ref name="swcw" />

==Behind the scenes==
Garu was first mentioned in the [[2001]] [[Star Wars Roleplaying Game|roleplaying]] supplement ''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'', by [[Bill Slavicsek]] and [[J.D. Wiker]].<ref name="Dark Side" /> The [[2007]] reference book [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] contradicts itself on when Garu lost the Holocron of King Adas, as it both said after the fall of the Sith Empire and just prior to the Great Hyperspace War.<ref name="Jedi" /> The [[2008]] reference book ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' confirmed Garu lost the holocron and died during the Great Hyperspace War. ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia'' also erroneusly claims Garu was a [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], or ''[[Jen'ari]]''.<ref name="CSWE" />
